mnemonics.title: Inheritance
mnemonics.content: Inheritance is a concept in object-oriented programming that allows one class to inherit the properties and behavior of another class.

mnemonics.title: Polymorphism
mnemonics.content: Polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms.

mnemonics.title: Encapsulation
mnemonics.content: Encapsulation is the concept of hiding the implementation details of an object from the outside world.

mnemonics.title: Abstraction
mnemonics.content: Abstraction is the concept of showing only the necessary information to the outside world while hiding the implementation details.

mnemonics.title: Composition
mnemonics.content: Composition is the concept of creating an object from other objects.

acronyms.usage: OOP is a programming paradigm that uses objects and classes to create software.
An API is a set of rules and protocols that allows different software systems to communicate with each other.
An IDE is a software application that provides a comprehensive development environment for writing, debugging, and testing code.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a family tree where each class is a node and the inheritance relationships are the branches.
Imagine a shape-shifter that can change its form to adapt to different situations.
Imagine a box with a lock and key where the implementation details are hidden inside the box and only the necessary information is exposed through the key.
Imagine a map that shows only the important landmarks and hides the unnecessary details.
Imagine building with blocks where each block represents an object and the composition of blocks creates a new object.

analogies.analogy: A child inheriting the traits of their parents.
An actor playing different roles in a play.
A safe that protects valuable items from the outside world.
A summary of a long document that highlights only the important points.
A recipe that combines different ingredients to create a new dish.
